title: rubus asper var. myriadenus
intro: Rubus asper var. myriadenus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant belonging to the rose family and native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid. The thimbleberry has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and sore throats, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Thimbleberry can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
